title: ExPASy

is an online [bioinformatics](/wiki/Bioinformatics "Bioinformatics") resource operated by the [SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics](/wiki/Swiss_Institute_of_Bioinformatics "Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics"). It is an [extensible](/wiki/Extensible "Extensible") and [integrative](/wiki/Integrative_bioinformatics "Integrative bioinformatics") portal which provides access to over 160 databases and software tools and supports a range of life science and clinical research areas, from [genomics](/wiki/Genomics "Genomics"), [proteomics](/wiki/Proteomics "Proteomics") and [structural biology](/wiki/Structural_biology "Structural biology"), to [evolution](/wiki/Evolution "Evolution") and [phylogeny](/wiki/Phylogenetics "Phylogenetics"), [systems biology](/wiki/Systems_biology "Systems biology") and [medical chemistry](/wiki/Medicinal_chemistry "Medicinal chemistry"). The individual resources (databases, web-based and downloadable software tools) are hosted in a decentralized way by different groups of the SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics and partner institutions.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [ExPASy](/w/index.php?title=ExPASy&redirect=no "ExPASy"))

Expert Protein-Analysis System

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/fc/Expasy_logo_Homepage.png/330px-Expasy_logo_Homepage.png)](/wiki/File:Expasy_logo_Homepage.png)

Logo Expasy 2020

**Expasy** is an online [bioinformatics](/wiki/Bioinformatics "Bioinformatics") resource operated by the [SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics](/wiki/Swiss_Institute_of_Bioinformatics "Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics"). It is an [extensible](/wiki/Extensible "Extensible") and [integrative](/wiki/Integrative_bioinformatics "Integrative bioinformatics") portal which provides access to over 160 databases and software tools and supports a range of life science and clinical research areas, from [genomics](/wiki/Genomics "Genomics"), [proteomics](/wiki/Proteomics "Proteomics") and [structural biology](/wiki/Structural_biology "Structural biology"), to [evolution](/wiki/Evolution "Evolution") and [phylogeny](/wiki/Phylogenetics "Phylogenetics"), [systems biology](/wiki/Systems_biology "Systems biology") and [medical chemistry](/wiki/Medicinal_chemistry "Medicinal chemistry"). The individual resources (databases, web-based and downloadable software tools) are hosted in a decentralized way by different groups of the SIB Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics and partner institutions.

## Search engine

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Expasy&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Search engine")]

Queries of Expasy allow:

1. parallel searches SIB databases through a single search
2. aggregated search results from the complete set of >160 resources accessible from the portal.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Expasy provides up-to-date information from the most recent release of each resources.

The terms used in Expasy are based on the EDAM comprehensive [ontology](/wiki/Ontology_(information_science) "Ontology (information science)").[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Expasy&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

Expasy was created in August 1993. Originally, it was called **ExPASy** (**Ex**pert **P**rotein **A**nalysis **Sy**stem) and acted as a [proteomics](/wiki/Proteomics "Proteomics") server to analyze [protein](/wiki/Protein "Protein") sequences and structures and [two-dimensional gel electrophoresis](/wiki/Two-dimensional_gel_electrophoresis "Two-dimensional gel electrophoresis") (2-D Page electrophoresis).[[3]](#cite_note-pmid12824418-3) Among others, ExPASy hosted the protein sequence knowledge base, [UniProtKB/Swiss-Prot](/wiki/Swiss-Prot "Swiss-Prot"), and its computer annotated supplement, UniProtKB/TrEMBL.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

ExPASy was the [first website](/wiki/List_of_websites_founded_before_1995 "List of websites founded before 1995") of the [life sciences](/wiki/Life_sciences "Life sciences") and among the first 150 websites in the world. As of 5 April 2007[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Expasy&action=edit), ExPASy had been consulted 1 billion times since its installation on 1 August 1993.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

In June 2011, it became the SIB ExPASy Bioinformatics Resources Portal: a diverse catalogue of bioinformatics resources developed by SIB Groups.[[6]](#cite_note-6) The current version of Expasy was released in October 2020.[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

| Software | * [BLAST](/wiki/BLAST_(biotechnology) "BLAST (biotechnology)") * [Bowtie](/wiki/Bowtie_(sequence_analysis) "Bowtie (sequence analysis)") * [Clustal](/wiki/Clustal "Clustal") * [EMBOSS](/wiki/EMBOSS "EMBOSS") * [HMMER](/wiki/HMMER "HMMER") * [MUSCLE](/wiki/MUSCLE_(alignment_software) "MUSCLE (alignment software)") * [PANGOLIN](/wiki/Phylogenetic_Assignment_of_Named_Global_Outbreak_Lineages "Phylogenetic Assignment of Named Global Outbreak Lineages") * [SAMtools](/wiki/SAMtools "SAMtools") * [SOAP suite](/wiki/Short_Oligonucleotide_Analysis_Package "Short Oligonucleotide Analysis Package") * [TopHat](/wiki/TopHat_(bioinformatics) "TopHat (bioinformatics)") |
| Other | * Server: [ExPASy](/wiki/ExPASy "ExPASy") * [Rosalind (education platform)](/wiki/Rosalind_(education_platform) "Rosalind (education platform)") |
| Institutions | * [Broad Institute](/wiki/Broad_Institute "Broad Institute") * [Computational Biology Department](/wiki/Computational_Biology_Department "Computational Biology Department") (CBD) * [Microsoft Research - University of Trento Centre for Computational and Systems Biology](/wiki/COSBI "COSBI") (COSBI) * [Database Center for Life Science](/wiki/Database_Center_for_Life_Science "Database Center for Life Science") (DBCLS) * [DNA Data Bank of Japan](/wiki/DNA_Data_Bank_of_Japan "DNA Data Bank of Japan") (DDBJ) * [European Bioinformatics Institute](/wiki/European_Bioinformatics_Institute "European Bioinformatics Institute") (EMBL-EBI) * [European Molecular Biology Laboratory](/wiki/European_Molecular_Biology_Laboratory "European Molecular Biology Laboratory") (EMBL) * [Flatiron Institute](/wiki/Flatiron_Institute "Flatiron Institute") * [J. Craig Venter Institute](/wiki/J._Craig_Venter_Institute "J. Craig Venter Institute") (JCVI) * [Joint Genome Institute](/wiki/Joint_Genome_Institute "Joint Genome Institute") (JGI) * [Max Planck Institute of Molecular Cell Biology and Genetics](/wiki/Max_Planck_Institute_of_Molecular_Cell_Biology_and_Genetics "Max Planck Institute of Molecular Cell Biology and Genetics") (MPI-CBG) * [US National Center for Biotechnology Information](/wiki/National_Center_for_Biotechnology_Information "National Center for Biotechnology Information") (NCBI) * [Japanese Institute of Genetics](/wiki/National_Institute_of_Genetics "National Institute of Genetics") * [Netherlands Bioinformatics Centre](/wiki/Netherlands_Bioinformatics_Centre "Netherlands Bioinformatics Centre") (NBIC) * [Philippine Genome Center](/wiki/Philippine_Genome_Center "Philippine Genome Center") (PGC) * [Scripps Research](/wiki/Scripps_Research "Scripps Research") * [Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics](/wiki/Swiss_Institute_of_Bioinformatics "Swiss Institute of Bioinformatics") (SIB) * [Wellcome Sanger Institute](/wiki/Wellcome_Sanger_Institute "Wellcome Sanger Institute") * [Whitehead Institute](/wiki/Whitehead_Institute "Whitehead Institute") |
| Organizations | * [African Society for Bioinformatics and Computational Biology](/wiki/African_Society_for_Bioinformatics_and_Computational_Biology "African Society for Bioinformatics and Computational Biology") (ASBCB) * [Australia Bioinformatics Resource](/wiki/Australia_Bioinformatics_Resource "Australia Bioinformatics Resource") (EMBL-AR) * [European Molecular Biology network](/wiki/EMBnet "EMBnet") (EMBnet) * [International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration](/wiki/International_Nucleotide_Sequence_Database_Collaboration "International Nucleotide Sequence Database Collaboration") (INSDC) * [International Society for Biocuration](/wiki/International_Society_for_Biocuration "International Society for Biocuration") (ISB) * [International Society for Computational Biology](/wiki/International_Society_for_Computational_Biology "International Society for Computational Biology") (ISCB)   + [Student Council](/wiki/International_Society_for_Computational_Biology_Student_Council "International Society for Computational Biology Student Council") (ISCB-SC) * [Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology](/wiki/Institute_of_Genomics_and_Integrative_Biology "Institute of Genomics and Integrative Biology") (CSIR-IGIB) * [Japanese Society for Bioinformatics](/wiki/Japanese_Society_for_Bioinformatics "Japanese Society for Bioinformatics") (JSBi) |
| Meetings | * Basel Computational Biology Conference‎ ([BC2]) * [European Conference on Computational Biology](/wiki/European_Conference_on_Computational_Biology "European Conference on Computational Biology") (ECCB) * [Intelligent Systems for Molecular Biology](/wiki/Intelligent_Systems_for_Molecular_Biology "Intelligent Systems for Molecular Biology") (ISMB) * [International Conference on Bioinformatics](/wiki/International_Conference_on_Bioinformatics "International Conference on Bioinformatics") (InCoB) * [International Conference on Computational Intelligence Methods for Bioinformatics and Biostatistics](/wiki/International_Conference_on_Computational_Intelligence_Methods_for_Bioinformatics_and_Biostatistics "International Conference on Computational Intelligence Methods for Bioinformatics and Biostatistics") (CIBB) * [ISCB Africa ASBCB Conference on Bioinformatics](/wiki/ISCB_Africa_ASBCB_Conference_on_Bioinformatics "ISCB Africa ASBCB Conference on Bioinformatics") * [Pacific Symposium on Biocomputing](/wiki/Pacific_Symposium_on_Biocomputing "Pacific Symposium on Biocomputing") (PSB) * [Research in Computational Molecular Biology](/wiki/Research_in_Computational_Molecular_Biology "Research in Computational Molecular Biology") (RECOMB) |
